sentences.base_language: I've been thinking about starting a small online shop.
sentences.target_language: 我一直在想开一个小网店。
sentences.romanized: Wǒ yīzhí zài xiǎng kāi yīgè xiǎo wǎngdiàn.
sentences.grammarNote:
**一直在** + verb describes an ongoing action over a period of time: 'have been doing...' It's the natural way to express something you've been thinking about or working on continuously.
**网店** (wǎngdiàn) = **网** (wǎng, internet/net) + **店** (diàn, shop/store) → online shop. **网** appears in many internet-related words: 网络 (wǎngluò, network), 网站 (wǎngzhàn, website), 上网 (shàngwǎng, go online).
**开** (kāi) = to open. 开店 = open a shop. **开** is very versatile: 开车 (drive), 开会 (hold a meeting), 开始 (start).

sentences.base_language: Really? That's a great idea! What would you sell?
sentences.target_language: 真的吗？好主意！你想卖什么？
sentences.romanized: Zhēn de ma? Hǎo zhǔyi! Nǐ xiǎng mài shénme?
sentences.grammarNote:
**真的吗** (zhēn de ma) = Really? — **真** (true/real) + **的** (particle) + **吗** (question). A very natural way to express surprise or seek confirmation.
**主意** (zhǔyi) = idea/plan: **主** (master/main) + **意** (idea/intention). 好主意 = good idea! Note the neutral-tone **yi** when unstressed.
**什么** (shénme) = what. In 你想卖什么, the question word stays in its natural position (where the answer goes): 你想卖手工品 → 你想卖什么?

sentences.base_language: Handmade things — bags, accessories, that sort of thing.
sentences.target_language: 手工的东西，包包、配饰什么的。
sentences.romanized: Shǒugōng de dōngxi, bāobāo, pèishì shénme de.
sentences.grammarNote:
**什么的** (shénme de) at the end of a list means 'and so on / and stuff like that.' Casual equivalents: **等等** (děngděng) or **之类的** (zhī lèi de).
**手工** (shǒugōng) = **手** (hand) + **工** (craft/work) → handmade/handicraft. The **的** after it links to the noun: 手工的东西 = handmade things.
**包包** (bāobāo) — reduplication of **包** (bag) makes it more casual and cute. Similar to how 妈妈 (mā, mother → māma) softens the word.

sentences.base_language: I think you'd be really good at that! Do you have a plan?
sentences.target_language: 我觉得你肯定做得好！有计划吗？
sentences.romanized: Wǒ juéde nǐ kěndìng zuò de hǎo! Yǒu jìhuà ma?
sentences.grammarNote:
**做得好** = do [something] well. The **得** (de) here is a structural particle linking the verb to a degree complement: verb + **得** + result/evaluation. 做得好 (do well), 说得快 (speak fast), 写得漂亮 (write beautifully).
**肯定** (kěndìng) = definitely/certainly. Works as an adverb (肯定会, will definitely) or verb (我肯定这件事, I confirm this).
**觉得** (juéde) = think/feel (an opinion): 我觉得… = I think / I feel…

sentences.base_language: Sort of. But honestly, I'm a little scared of failing.
sentences.target_language: 有一些。但说实话，我有点害怕失败。
sentences.romanized: Yǒu yīxiē. Dàn shuō shíhuà, wǒ yǒudiǎn hàipà shībài.
sentences.grammarNote:
**说实话** (shuō shíhuà) = honestly speaking / to tell the truth. A conversational opener used to signal that you're sharing a real, sometimes vulnerable, feeling.
**有点** (yǒudiǎn) = a bit / slightly. Used before adjectives/emotional states to soften them: 有点累 (a bit tired), 有点担心 (a bit worried). Note: **一点** is used after adjectives (大一点, a bit bigger); **有点** comes before.
**害怕** (hàipà) = **害** (harm/dread) + **怕** (fear) → be afraid. Both characters relate to fear: 我怕 = I'm scared, 害怕失败 = afraid of failing.

sentences.base_language: That feeling is totally normal. Everyone is scared at the start.
sentences.target_language: 这种感觉很正常。谁开始的时候都会怕。
sentences.romanized: Zhè zhǒng gǎnjué hěn zhèngcháng. Shéi kāishǐ de shíhou dōu huì pà.
sentences.grammarNote:
**这种** (zhè zhǒng) = **这** (this) + **种** (zhǒng, kind/type) → this kind of. **种** is the measure word for types and varieties: 这种感觉 (this kind of feeling), 三种方法 (three kinds of methods).
**谁…都** = 'whoever… all / everyone…': 谁开始的时候都会怕 = whoever starts (anyone who starts) will be afraid. **谁、什么、哪** + **都** = any…/every…: 什么都好 (anything is fine), 哪里都行 (anywhere works).
**正常** (zhèngcháng) = **正** (correct/upright) + **常** (ordinary/normal) → normal.

sentences.base_language: What if I put in all this effort and it doesn't work out?
sentences.target_language: 万一我努力了，结果还是不成功呢？
sentences.romanized: Wànyī wǒ nǔlì le, jiéguǒ háishì bù chénggōng ne?
sentences.grammarNote:
**万一** (wànyī) = 'what if / just in case something goes wrong.' It implies you're worried about a bad outcome.
**要是** / **如果** are more neutral 'if'; 万一 carries a sense of risk or an unlikely-but-worrying scenario.
**努力** (nǔlì) = **努** (exert) + **力** (force/effort) → work hard / put in effort. Also an adjective: 很努力 (very hardworking).
**呢** (ne) at the end of a question adds a wondering, contemplative tone — like thinking out loud: 那怎么办呢 (then what should I do?).

sentences.base_language: Even if it doesn't succeed, you'll learn a lot. That's never wasted.
sentences.target_language: 就算不成功，你也会学到很多。那不会白费的。
sentences.romanized: Jiùsuàn bù chénggōng, nǐ yě huì xuédào hěn duō. Nà bù huì báifèi de.
sentences.grammarNote:
**就算...也** = even if... still... It concedes a possibility while keeping the main point: 就算下雨也去 = even if it rains, I'll still go. A stronger version of **即使...也**.
**学到** (xuédào) = **学** (learn) + **到** (arrive at/reach) → learn and gain (successfully). The **到** is a result complement showing the learning was effective: 学到很多 (learned a lot from it).
**白费** (báifèi) = **白** (white/in vain) + **费** (spend/waste) → waste for nothing. **白** meaning 'in vain' appears in: 白费力气 (waste effort), 白说 (said for nothing).

sentences.base_language: You're right. I should just try. Dreams don't come true on their own.
sentences.target_language: 你说得对。我应该试试。梦想不会自己实现的。
sentences.romanized: Nǐ shuō de duì. Wǒ yīnggāi shì shi. Mèngxiǎng bù huì zìjǐ shíxiàn de.
sentences.grammarNote:
**说得对** = **说** (say) + **得** (de, result particle) + **对** (correct) → said correctly / you're right. The **得** pattern links a verb to its result: 说得好 (said well), 做得对 (done correctly), 来得早 (arrived early).
**试试** = **试** + **试** — verb reduplication meaning 'give it a try / try a little'. Very common for encouraging someone to have a go: 试试吧 (just give it a try).
**实现** (shíxiàn) = **实** (real/actual) + **现** (appear/realize) → to realize/achieve (a goal or dream). 梦想实现 = a dream comes true.

sentences.base_language: Exactly! Start small, take it one step at a time. You've got this!
sentences.target_language: 就是！从小做起，一步一步来。加油！
sentences.romanized: Jiù shì! Cóng xiǎo zuò qǐ, yī bù yī bù lái. Jiāyóu!
sentences.grammarNote:
**一步一步来** = take it one step at a time. This reduplication pattern (one + measure + one + measure) expresses doing something gradually:
一天一天 (day by day), 一点一点 (little by little).
**就是** (jiù shì) = exactly! / precisely! Used to strongly agree with what was just said. Context-dependent: it can also mean 'it's just that…' (introducing a caveat): 就是有点贵 (it's just a bit expensive).
**从小做起** = **从** (from) + **小** (small) + **做** (do) + **起** (start) → start from small / begin small. **从…做起** is a useful frame: 从基础做起 (start from the basics).
